rationale: The citing paper references Khan and Dhar (2006) only once in a grouped citation when discussing a possible negative spillover mechanism: households might feel licensed to indulge in less healthy food at home. The paper does not develop hypotheses from this specific study, operationalize its mechanism, or use it to design analyses. Later interpretation mentions licensing as one of several possible explanations but explicitly states the data cannot identify mechanisms. This indicates a background mention rather than a substantive dependence on the cited paper.
